questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the given figure, the angle of inclination of the inclined plane is $30^{\circ}$. The horizontal velocity $V_0$ so that the particle hits the inclined plane perpendicularly is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & v_y=\sqrt{2 g \mathrm{H}} \\ & \frac{v_y}{v_x}=\frac{\sqrt{2 g H}}{v_0}=\tan 60^{\circ}=\sqrt{3} \\ & v_0=\sqrt{\frac{2 g H}{3}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

A particle of mass $m$ is projected from O with velocity $\mathrm{v}_0 \cos \theta \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\mathrm{v}_0 \sin \theta \hat{\mathbf{j}}$.
At the same instant, another particle of mass 2 m is projected from point $P$ with $v_0 \hat{k}$. The velocity of $2^{\text {nd }}$ particle w.r.t first particle after time $t=\frac{v_0 \sin \theta}{g}$ is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle is placed in a cart. Initial location of the particle is taken as origin in ground frame. At $t=0$, the cart starts moving along $x$-axis with uniform velocity $2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and at the same instant the particle starts moving with $2 x \hat{j}$ relative to cart where $x$ is $x$-coordinate of the cart. Which of the following is the radius of curvature of the trajectory of the particle in ground frame when it's tangential acceleration is equal to normal acceleration in magnitude?
questions.solution:
$\vec{V}_{P G}=\vec{V}_{P C}+\vec{V}_{C G}=2 x \hat{j}+2 \hat{i}$
Hence, for the paritcle, $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{x}}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{y}}=2 \mathrm{x}=4 \mathrm{t}$
$\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{x}}=0 \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{y}}=4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
$\mathrm{x}=2 \mathrm{t}, \mathrm{y}=2 \mathrm{t}^2$
$\Rightarrow a_{\text {Total }}=4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
(a) (along Y-axis)
and trajectory $\Rightarrow y=2 \cdot \frac{x^2}{4}=\frac{x^2}{2}$
when $\left|\vec{a}_\tau\right|=\left|\vec{a}_N\right|, a \cos \theta=a \sin \theta \Rightarrow \theta=45^{\circ}$
So, $V_x=V_y=2 \Rightarrow V=2 \sqrt{2}$
$\therefore R_C=\frac{\mathrm{V}^2}{\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{N}}}=\frac{4 \times 2}{4 \cdot \cos 45^{\circ}}=2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m}=\frac{4}{\sqrt{2}} \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A swimmer can swim in still water with a speed of $\sqrt{5} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. While crossing a river his average speed is $3 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. If he cross the river in the shortest possible time, what is the speed of flow of water?
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$\mathbf{S}_1$ : Two particles are in projectile motion at small height under gravity. The path of one particle as seen by another particle will be a straight line.
$\mathrm{S}_2$ : If $\frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=0$ at $\mathrm{x}=\mathrm{x}_1$ and $\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{dx}^2} \lt 0$ at $\mathrm{x}=\mathrm{x}_1$ then v has maximum value at $\mathrm{x}=\mathrm{x}_1$.
$\mathbf{S}_3$ : In order to cross a river of uniform width (flow is also uniform) in shortest time, swimmer must swim in perpendicular direction of river flow.
$\mathbf{S}_4$ : If acceleration of particle is positive then its speed increase.
State, in order, whether $\mathrm{S}_1, \mathrm{~S}_2, \mathrm{~S}_3, \mathrm{~S}_4$ are true or false
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{S}_1 \rightarrow$ True $\quad$ Both are falling under gravity.
$\mathrm{S}_2 \rightarrow$ False Function has maxima at $x=x_1$, not necessary maximum value also.
$\mathrm{S}_3 \rightarrow$ True $\mathrm{T}=\frac{\mathrm{W}}{\mathrm{V}}$ to minimize $\mathrm{T}, \mathrm{V}$ will be maximum.
i.e whole effort of swimmer must towards opposite bank.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
